messages.content: You are the Host Forensics Expert. Target OS: Windows. Vector Space: 4D windows_math. Source: Sysmon event stream. Schema: sysmon_event_id, Image, CommandLine, ParentImage, User, IntegrityLevel, TargetImage, GrantedAccess, TargetObject, Details, ImageLoaded, Signed, SignatureStatus, TargetFilename, TamperingType, EventType_reg. Identify defense evasion tradecraft. Output MITRE ATT&CK + containment.

Spatial Anomaly Detected.
Source: sysmon_sensor
Vector: <|spatial_vector|>
Windows Host — AMSI In-Process Memory Patch.
Host: SRV-24  User: rbrown
  Process: wscript.exe (PID 8007)
  target_function: amsi.dll!AmsiScanBuffer
  patch_method: conditional jump NOP/JMP patch
  patch_pattern: AMSI_TEST_JZ: 85 C0 74 → 85 C0 EB
  patch_bytes: 0x31 0xC0 0xC3
  VirtualProtect_sequence: PAGE_EXECUTE_READ → PAGE_EXECUTE_READWRITE → PAGE_EXECUTE_READ
  write_operations_to_amsi_text_section=6
  obfuscated_snippet_loaded_before_commands=YES

<analysis>
[AXIS 1] Benign Alternative Assessment:
  Windows Defender and security vendors read amsi.dll for scanning but never write to its .text section at runtime. A wscript.exe process modifying AmsiScanBuffer instruction bytes has no legitimate use case.
[AXIS 2] Behavioral Proof Assessment:
  VirtualProtect sequence on amsi.dll .text: RX→RW (write enabled) → patch written → RX (restored). Patch bytes 0x31 0xC0 0xC3 at AmsiScanBuffer: JMP = skip scan logic. Method: conditional jump NOP/JMP patch. Obfuscated bypass snippet executed before main script — covers tracks. 
[AXIS 3] Entity Coverage:
  Host SRV-24: AMSI is disabled for all scripts in the wscript.exe session. Malicious PowerShell/CLR code can now execute without AMSI scanning. Any malware loaded after this bypass runs without detection.
[CONCLUSION] In-process AMSI memory patch confirmed.
</analysis>
TRUE POSITIVE. MITRE T1562.001 (Disable or Modify Tools: AMSI bypass). Kill process, restore amsi.dll integrity, escalate to IR.
RECOMMENDED_ACTION: contain

Spatial Anomaly Detected.
Source: sysmon_sensor
Vector: <|spatial_vector|>
Windows Host — Defender AMSI Read Access.
  Process: MsMpEng.exe
  operation=READ_ONLY memory scan  no_VirtualProtect_write=YES
  signed=True  amsi_dll_integrity=INTACT

<analysis>
[AXIS 1] Benign Alternative Assessment:
  MsMpEng.exe reading amsi.dll for scanning — read-only access, no .text modification.
[AXIS 2] Behavioral Proof Assessment:
  No VirtualProtect write sequence. amsi.dll .text section unchanged. Defender read-only.
[AXIS 3] Entity Coverage:
  Authorized Defender AMSI scanning — read-only, signed.
[CONCLUSION] Authorized Defender AMSI usage. No action.
</analysis>
FALSE POSITIVE. T1562.001 — AUTHORIZED DEFENDER OPERATION. No action.
RECOMMENDED_ACTION: dismiss
